title: Procedural justice for whom? A case study of a densification project
abstract: The study examines resident perceptions of justice and participation in a neighborhood densification project within an existing urban district.

title: Critical success factors for the implementation of urban regeneration REITs
abstract: This paper identifies financing and governance factors shaping implementation of urban regeneration REITs in existing built-up areas.

title: Street regeneration for thermal comfort in an old district
abstract: The paper evaluates how redesigning and regenerating streets in an old district affects habitability and resilience.

title: Social network conservation in historical urban areas
abstract: This article studies conservation-led renewal in historical urban areas and its effects on resident social networks.

title: Blight intervention in shrinking cities: demolition or rehabilitation?
abstract: The study compares demolition and rehabilitation policies for vacant properties in declining neighborhoods.

title: State-led gentrification and displacement under urban renewal
abstract: The article analyzes how state-supported renewal programs produce displacement and self-respect harms among existing residents.

title: Brownfield waterfront redevelopment and coalition politics
abstract: This paper examines redevelopment of an abandoned waterfront brownfield through public-private coalition building.

title: Relocation outcomes in an urban village renewal project
abstract: The study evaluates relocation, compensation, and neighborhood change after a specific urban village renewal intervention.

title: Historic district revitalization through adaptive reuse
abstract: The article focuses on revitalization of a historic district through adaptive reuse and conservation-oriented planning.

title: Implementation pathways for old community renewal
abstract: This paper studies policy tools, delivery mechanisms, and resident participation in old community renewal programs.

title: Exploring lay views on physical activity and public health in East Belfast
abstract: Using an urban regeneration project in East Belfast, this paper evaluates how regeneration affects physical activity and community health.

title: The impact of urban renewal on land surface temperature changes
abstract: The paper uses multi-source remote sensing to evaluate temperature changes caused by urban renewal in Guangzhou.

title: Indian Bus Rapid Transit Systems Funded by the Jawaharlal Nehru National Urban Renewal Mission
abstract: This paper reviews planning, implementation, and operation of bus rapid transit systems funded under a national urban renewal mission.

title: Optimization of drainage systems for flooding mitigation in an urban renewal project
abstract: The study proposes an optimization model for drainage and impervious surfaces within a concrete urban renewal case.

title: Automatic identification of bottlenecks for ambulance passage on urban streets
abstract: This article develops a deep learning method to identify ambulance bottlenecks as a key task in urban renewal and street governance initiatives.

title: Urban Built Environment Assessment Based on Scene Understanding of High-Resolution Remote Sensing Imagery
abstract: The paper proposes a built-environment assessment method and explicitly frames it as decision support for urban renewal improvement.

title: Bridging heritage conservation and energy efficiency in historic social housing
abstract: This study presents a retrofitting framework for historic social housing and positions it as an urban regeneration model for municipalities.

title: Pocket park design as part of inner-city regeneration
abstract: The paper evaluates pocket park design and social value in a named inner-city regeneration project.

title: Area-based implementation of urban regeneration policy in Poland
abstract: The article studies how urban regeneration policy is implemented across functional urban areas and what governance bottlenecks emerge.

title: Property-led regeneration and limits of redevelopment coalitions
abstract: The study analyzes property-led regeneration, local authorities, and redevelopment coalitions in affected neighborhoods.

title: Re-urbanisation in regenerated areas of Manchester and Glasgow
abstract: The article examines new residents and sustainability problems in regenerated areas of Manchester and Glasgow.

title: Housing rights regimes amid urban renewal in Shanghai and Mumbai
abstract: This article examines housing rights, demolitions, and activism within ongoing urban renewal in Shanghai and Mumbai.

title: Evaluation of environmental quality after redevelopment of an industrial waterfront
abstract: The study evaluates environmental quality and resident experience after redevelopment of a named industrial waterfront district.

title: Renewal law and seismic upgrading decisions in Turkish neighborhoods
abstract: The paper analyzes how the urban renewal law shapes neighborhood-level seismic upgrading and redevelopment decisions.

title: Creative industry relocation in a docklands regeneration program
abstract: The article evaluates relocation outcomes and workspace loss in a named docklands regeneration program.

title: Appropriate public-realm design for the blind and partially sighted
abstract: The article evaluates accessibility-oriented public-realm design in regenerated city centers and treats regeneration-led street change as the direct object.

title: Urban regeneration and sustainable communities: the role of networks, innovation, and creativity
abstract: This paper studies partnerships, innovation, and governance mechanisms inside urban regeneration programs and evaluates their role in sustainable communities.

title: Beautiful urbanism: gender, landscape, and contestation in Latino Chicago's age of urban renewal
abstract: The paper analyzes how urban renewal reshaped landscape, gendered experience, and contestation in Latino Chicago, treating renewal as the central process under study.

title: Urban heat island dynamics from the perspective of urban renewal
abstract: The paper analyzes long-run UHI trends and thermal gradients across cities and only frames the issue as relevant to renewal planning.

title: Predictive fire models for renewal districts
abstract: A machine learning model predicts fire occurrence and may support decision making in renewal areas, but the contribution is methodological.

title: Urban sustainability indicators for future scenarios
abstract: This paper develops sustainability indicators for future urban scenarios without any concrete renewal intervention as the object.

title: The hotel and the city
abstract: The article surveys hotels as part of twentieth-century urbanism and only notes that some were connected to renewal strategies.

title: Underground pedestrian systems for urban sustainability
abstract: The study examines underground pedestrian systems as a mobility and sustainability phenomenon, not as a renewal intervention chain.

title: Black-Friendly businesses in cities during the Civil Rights Era
abstract: This is a historical study of Black business districts and civil-rights-era urban change; renewal is mentioned only as context.

title: Archaeological reconstruction of an early mosque
abstract: The paper reconstructs an early mosque and attributes it to a historical wave of urban renewal in the eighth century.

title: Optimization of redevelopment candidate blocks using explainable AI
abstract: The main contribution is an optimization method for block selection; renewal is primarily the application scenario for the algorithm.

title: Concrete performance for recycled demolition waste
abstract: The paper studies material properties of concrete using waste from renewal and demolition sites, but not renewal itself.

title: Using demolition wastes from urban regeneration as sensible thermal energy storage material
abstract: The paper evaluates thermal storage behavior of demolition wastes from regeneration sites; urban regeneration is only the waste source, not the study object.

title: Segregated neighborhoods and federal policy in the twentieth century
abstract: This article examines segregation and federal housing policy over time without analyzing a concrete renewal intervention.

title: Persistent organic pollutants in the topsoil of a typical urban renewal area
abstract: This study maps pollutant distribution and carcinogenic risk in soils from a typical urban renewal area, but does not study renewal itself.

title: Distribution and health risk assessment of organochlorine pesticides in industrial site soils
abstract: The article evaluates organochlorine pesticide risk in a contaminated industrial site described as part of urban renewal in Beijing.

title: The Three Santiago Calatrava Bridges in Reggio Emilia, Italy
abstract: The paper describes bridge structures and only remarks that the bridges played an important role in urban regeneration.

title: Gentrification in Hong Kong? Epistemology vs. Ontology
abstract: This article debates the concept of gentrification in Hong Kong without studying a concrete renewal intervention chain.

title: Street-level bureaucrats coping with conflicts in area-based initiatives
abstract: The paper studies participatory planning processes and street-level bureaucrats in area-based initiatives, mentioning regeneration only broadly.

title: Culture-led place-making and brand building between Ljubljana and Porto
abstract: The article compares place-making and branding strategies in two cities and discusses adaptive reuse only at a broad placemaking level.

title: Men at the margin: Masculinity and space in downtown Vancouver, 1950-1986
abstract: The paper studies masculinity and social construction of urban space and says those narratives once provided a rationale for urban renewal.

title: Engineering community spirit in Dutch local governance
abstract: This article analyzes community participation and affective citizenship in a deprived neighborhood without a concrete renewal anchor.

title: Housing God, Losing Ground in urban China
abstract: The paper examines church responses to demolition and development politics but does not analyze renewal as the study object.

title: Visual and aesthetic markers of gentrification in tourist destinations
abstract: The study maps aesthetic markers of tourism-led gentrification without analyzing a specific regeneration intervention chain.

title: Delivering shared-space schemes: two case studies from Bournemouth
abstract: The paper describes design and consultation for shared-space schemes but does not situate them inside a renewal project or renewal policy.

title: Monthly monitoring of urban development and renewal at block level using Sentinel-2 time series
abstract: The contribution is a block-level remote sensing method for urban development monitoring; renewal is broad framing rather than the concrete object.

title: A cloud-edge architecture to support post-earthquake reconstruction
abstract: The paper proposes a technical architecture for post-earthquake reconstruction and does not study any urban renewal intervention chain.

title: Residents' attitudes toward future tourism development and community attachment
abstract: The article studies tourism development, well-being, and attachment, with no concrete regeneration or renewal anchor.

title: Unpacking the 15-Minute City via 6G, IoT, and Digital Twins
abstract: This paper develops a digital vision for efficiency and resilience in cities and does not analyze a renewal project or renewal policy.

title: Creative cities: The role of culture
abstract: The article discusses culture-led urban development and occasionally references regeneration, but not a concrete renewal intervention.

title: The global cultural city? Politics in multicultural marketplaces of South-east Asia
abstract: The paper analyzes spatial imagineering and multicultural marketplaces, with urban renewal serving only as background context.

title: Poverty and disrepair in private sector housing
abstract: This article challenges myths about poor housing conditions but does not examine a renewal intervention or regeneration policy.

title: THE SEARCH FOR A CENTER - THE RECOMPOSITION OF RACE, CLASS AND SPACE IN LOS-ANGELES
abstract: The paper examines planning histories and postwar spatial restructuring in Los Angeles, including struggles against urban renewal as a central historical process.

title: From squatters to creatives. An innovation perspective on temporary use in planning
abstract: This article reconstructs how temporary use of vacant inner-city spaces became a strategically embedded planning procedure in Germany.

title: Controlling Images of Neighborhoods in Gentrification Coverage
abstract: The paper analyzes media representations of neighborhoods under gentrification and redevelopment as the main urban change process.

title: Citizen participation, agency and voice
abstract: The article studies participation in local public action, including urban renewal, and treats renewal participation as a substantive governance field rather than background context.

title: Structural changes in a Danish social housing area: The impact of forced permanent rehousing on contact frequency with general practitioner and use of antidepressants
abstract: This study measures health consequences of rehousing in a social housing area and does not analyze an urban renewal program as the main research object.

title: People living in ageing buildings: Their quality of life and sense of belonging
abstract: The paper evaluates quality of life and place belonging among residents of ageing buildings, even though the districts were designated for demolition under a renewal programme.

title: Urban renewal moves in dimer models
abstract: This paper studies bipartite graph tiling, combinatorics, and cluster algebra under the urban renewal move.

title: Governing property-led rural regeneration in Ireland
abstract: The article examines a rural renewal scheme and its implications for rural development in declining regions.

title: Deep learning architecture for UAV multispectral pollution monitoring
abstract: The framework combines remote sensing imagery, sensors, and optimization for pollution mapping and environmental monitoring.

title: Remote sensing framework for adaptive reuse in a historic district
abstract: This UAV multispectral study supports adaptive reuse and compensation planning in a historic district.
